Joint Motion To Stay Pending Arbitration
Off Calendar Feb-26-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. STEPHEN D. WEISSKOPF, Esq. of Levatolaw, LLP (Tel: 310-734-2026) for defendant Knotel, Inc.;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF SKILLZ, INC.'s Joint Motion To Stay Pending Arbitration.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Skillz, Inc. and defendant Knotel, Inc. jointly move to stay this case pending their arbitration; the motion is granted.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-13-2021 Set For Order To Show Cause On Mar-09-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-30-2020 Continued To Jan-27-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Aug-07-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Stephen Weisskopk, Esq. 310-779-2024 sweisskopf@levatolaw of Levato Law for Defendant Knotel.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested/Discussed. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF SKILLZ, INC.'s Motion For A Preliminary Injunction.

Plaintiff Skillz, Inc.'s motion for a preliminary injunction is denied.

Defendant Knotel, Inc. developed a workspace for Skillz in San Francisco, which was to be substantially complete by May 31, 2020. Knotel asserts that the workspace was substantially complete by then, Skillz that it was not. Skillz would enjoin Knotel from "using, disbursing, transferring dissipating or otherwise encumbering or alienating" a refundable deposit that Skillz made on the project.

Skillz has not carried its burden to show that it would prevail at trial. (Husain v. McDonald's Corp. (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 860, 866-67.) Knotel adduces evidence that, during a May 21, 2020 walkthrough, Skillz expressed no concern about the workplace's state and instead complimented Knotel's ability to persevere during the Covid-19 pandemic. (Morningstar Dec. Paras. 14-15.) In turn, Skillz's motion relies solely on its complaint's allegations.

Nor has Skillz shown that the balance of harms favors it. (Husain, 205 Cal.App.4th at 866-67.) Skillz argues that Knotel is financially troubled, relying solely on allegations "on information and belief." But such allegations cannot support an injunction. (Ancora-Citronelle Corp. v. Green (1974) 41 Cal.App.3d 146, 150 n.1.) Moreover, Knotel has provided a thorough declaration regarding its financial condition from its CFO. (Jureller Dec.)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Aug-07-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Stephen Weisskopk, Esq. 310-779-2024 sweisskopf@levatolaw of Levato Law for Defendant Knotel.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested/Discussed. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF SKILLZ, INC.'s MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE.

Skillz, Inc.'s unopposed motion to admit counsel Richard Briansky pro hac vice is granted.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
